name: Tomato Basil Soup
tagline: Roasted-tomato soup blended velvety with cream and torn basil

hero.alt: Bowl of velvety tomato soup with basil leaves and cream swirl
hero.caption: tomato basil — roasted and silky

steps.title: Roast the tomatoes
steps.detail: Halve tomatoes, scatter with onion wedges and garlic on a sheet pan. Drizzle olive oil, salt, pepper. Roast at 425°F for 30 minutes until edges are charred.
steps.tip: Don't crowd the pan — single layer or use two pans.

steps.title: Transfer and simmer
steps.detail: Scrape everything (juices included) into a pot. Add stock, bring to a simmer for 10 minutes.
steps.tip: Charred bits = flavor. Don't leave them on the pan.

steps.title: Blend velvety
steps.detail: Blend in batches (or with immersion blender) until silky. For restaurant-smooth, push through a sieve.
steps.tip: Hot soup + sealed blender = explosion. Vent the lid with a towel.

steps.title: Cream and basil
steps.detail: Stir in cream off heat. Tear in basil at the last second. Adjust salt. A pinch of sugar if tomatoes are sharp.
steps.tip: Adding basil off heat keeps it bright green and pungent.

healthFacts: Roasting tomatoes concentrates lycopene and dispels excess water.
Pairing with olive oil increases lycopene absorption — fat-soluble carotenoid.
